behavioral.name: Flipkart Culture Guide
behavioral.desc: Official careers page — understand Flipkart values

interview_reviews.question: Graph cycle detection + DP on strings
interview_reviews.quote: HackerRank OA, 90 min, 3 problems. Need to solve at least 2 fully. Medium-Hard difficulty.

interview_reviews.question: Design a parking lot system
interview_reviews.quote: 2 hours to build a working system. They evaluate SOLID, design patterns, extensibility. Code review follows.

interview_reviews.question: Merge intervals + Topological sort
interview_reviews.quote: 1 hard DSA problem. Must explain brute force first, then optimize. Dry-run expected.

interview_reviews.question: Project discussion + behavioral
interview_reviews.quote: 30 min project deep-dive + why Flipkart. Pretty relaxed — they want to see genuine interest.

insider_tips: Machine coding round is THE differentiator — practice 10+ problems
SOLID principles are non-negotiable — they review your code structure
Flipkart uses Java/Kotlin heavily — familiarity helps
OA is on HackerRank — practice there specifically
Code readability > cleverness — write clean, well-named code

dont: Write monolithic code in machine coding
Skip design patterns — they check for them

impressive_projects.title: Payment Gateway SDK
impressive_projects.desc: Build a payment processing SDK with retry logic, idempotency, and webhook notifications.

impressive_projects.title: Credit Score Analyzer
impressive_projects.desc: Dashboard showing credit score breakdown, trend analysis, and personalized improvement suggestions.

impressive_projects.title: Reward Points Engine
impressive_projects.desc: Design a modular rewards system with earning rules, redemption, expiry, and tier management.

must_have: Clean architecture and SOLID principles are mandatory
Fintech or payment domain experience is a huge plus
CRED values design excellence — beautiful code matters

interview_reviews.question: Build a modular CLI application with extensible command pattern
interview_reviews.quote: Take-home assignment. They evaluate code structure, patterns, and extensibility. No UI needed.

interview_reviews.question: Implement LFU Cache
interview_reviews.quote: Medium DSA + puzzle. Coded on Google Docs. They focus on problem-solving approach.

interview_reviews.question: Design a caching system with eviction policies
interview_reviews.quote: Clean, modular code with design patterns. Code review was intense — they questioned every design decision.

interview_reviews.question: Design a payment analytics SDK
interview_reviews.quote: HLD + LLD combined with managerial discussion. Cultural fit at CRED is about craftsmanship and excellence.

insider_tips: CRED values code craftsmanship above all — write beautiful, clean code
Machine coding is THE most important round
Know SOLID principles cold — they will grill you
Design patterns like Strategy, Observer, Factory are commonly tested
CRED's engineering bar is among the highest in India

dont: Skip SOLID principles in machine coding
Be overconfident — CRED's bar is high
Write code without proper error handling

impressive_projects.desc: Build a UPI-like payment system with P2P transfers, merchant payments, and transaction reconciliation.

impressive_projects.title: Payment Dashboard Analytics
impressive_projects.desc: Real-time dashboard showing transaction volumes, success rates, and settlement tracking.

must_have: Payment/fintech project is a major plus
Understanding of transaction safety and idempotency
API design skills are critical at Razorpay

interview_reviews.question: 15 MCQs (OS/DBMS/OOP) + 3 coding problems
interview_reviews.quote: MCQs + story-based coding. The coding problems are wrapped in real-world scenarios. 90 min total.

interview_reviews.question: Find median in two sorted arrays
interview_reviews.quote: One hard problem with follow-ups. Must explain brute → optimal. Time complexity discussion mandatory.

interview_reviews.question: Design an in-memory database with transactions
interview_reviews.quote: 2 hours to build working system. ACID properties tested. Clean code and extensibility matter most.

insider_tips: Razorpay OA includes MCQs on OS/DBMS — brush up on fundamentals
Story-based coding problems are unique to Razorpay — read them carefully
Machine coding is the deciding round — practice building full systems
Know basics of payment systems — UPI flows, settlement cycles
API design is a hot topic — know REST best practices

dont: Skip MCQ prep — it's a filter round
Rush through without reading story problems carefully

system_design.name: Machine Coding Prep
system_design.desc: PhonePe tests machine coding for SDE-2+

interview_reviews.question: 3-4 coding problems (easy to hard)
interview_reviews.quote: Standard OA with DSA focus. Need to solve at least 3/4. Time limit is tight.

interview_reviews.question: Shortest path in weighted graph + String DP
interview_reviews.quote: One hard problem with implementation expected. Clean code, proper complexity analysis.

interview_reviews.question: Cultural fit + career goals
interview_reviews.quote: Standard HR round. They want to know why PhonePe and your long-term plans.

insider_tips: PhonePe is Java/Kotlin heavy — language familiarity helps
DSA is the primary filter — solve 300+ LeetCode problems
Machine coding for SDE-2+: practice building complete systems
Know UPI architecture basics — NPCI, payment settlement flow
They value scalable solutions — always discuss scale

impressive_projects.title: Stock Portfolio Tracker
impressive_projects.desc: Real-time portfolio dashboard with P&L tracking, holdings analysis, and price alerts.

impressive_projects.title: Mutual Fund SIP Calculator
impressive_projects.desc: Interactive SIP planner with goal-based projections, tax estimation, and comparison charts.

interview_reviews.question: MCQs on time complexity + threading + 1 coding problem
interview_reviews.quote: Light OA. MCQs on fundamentals + one medium-level coding question. Not too hard.

interview_reviews.question: Array manipulation + puzzle (weight balance)
interview_reviews.quote: They guide you if stuck. Relaxed atmosphere. Focus on approach > perfect solution.

interview_reviews.question: Project deep-dive + career aspirations
interview_reviews.quote: Discussion about your best project, tech choices, what you learned. Very conversational.

insider_tips: Groww's interview is more relaxed than other unicorns
They value practical coding skills over competitive programming
For web roles: know React, HTML/CSS, JavaScript deeply
Investment domain knowledge is a plus but not required
They have a good work-life balance — show you value that too

do: Be honest about what you know/don't know
Be conversational in the HM round
dont: Ignore frontend fundamentals for web roles
Be fake about knowing stock markets
Underestimate the interview — it's still competitive

interview_reviews.question: 2-3 medium DSA: arrays, sliding window, HashMap
interview_reviews.quote: Standard OA. Focus on arrays and sliding window. SQL questions sometimes included.

interview_reviews.question: Cab booking system (LLD) + DP problem
interview_reviews.quote: Mix of LLD and DSA in one round. Design entities, APIs, handle concurrency.

interview_reviews.question: Past projects + API design (versioning, idempotency)
interview_reviews.quote: HM grills on API design. Know versioning, security headers, idempotency keys.

insider_tips: Swiggy mixes LLD with DSA in same rounds — be versatile
API design (versioning, idempotency) is a favourite HM topic
Know microservices vs monolith trade-offs
OA includes SQL sometimes — review basic queries
Competitive compensation — one of the best in Indian startups

dont: Be unprepared about Swiggy's tech stack

interview_reviews.question: 2-3 coding + MCQs on OS/CN/Java
interview_reviews.quote: Mix of coding + CS fundamentals MCQs. Medium difficulty. Need to clear both sections.

interview_reviews.question: Bipartite Graph + Trie implementation
interview_reviews.quote: Graph and Trie problems. Must implement from scratch. Edge cases tested.

interview_reviews.question: Car Pooling System
interview_reviews.quote: Design classes, implement core logic. OOP principles graded heavily.

insider_tips: Meesho OA includes CS fundamentals MCQs — prep OS, DBMS, CN
Machine coding round evaluates OOP — practice class design
Social commerce domain knowledge helps but isn't mandatory
They ask about graph algorithms frequently
Strong DSA fundamentals are the primary filter

do: Brush up CS fundamentals for MCQs
dont: Write procedural code in machine coding
Be unprepared about Meesho's business model

interview_reviews.question: BST operations + Maximum Product Subarray
interview_reviews.quote: 2 problems, easy-medium. They value speed and clean code over complex algos.

interview_reviews.question: Design a delivery allocation system
interview_reviews.quote: Focus on DB schema, third-party integrations, error handling. Real-world engineering.

interview_reviews.question: DB indexing, async problems, past experience

insider_tips: Zepto is fast-paced startup — show you can move quickly
DB knowledge (indexing, sharding) is heavily tested
Quick commerce domain knowledge is a plus
They value practical engineering over algorithmic complexity
Process is short — usually done in 1-2 weeks

interview_reviews.question: House Robber variation + Graph traversal
interview_reviews.quote: Story-based DSA problems. Read carefully. Standard DP + graph underneath.

interview_reviews.question: Design a rider onboarding system
interview_reviews.quote: SDE-2/3 interviewer. Discussed DB choices (SQL vs NoSQL), indexing, sharding, latency optimization.

interview_reviews.question: Previous experience + fast-paced work discussion
interview_reviews.quote: Blinkit is part of Zomato. Fast-paced culture. Show you thrive under pressure.

insider_tips: Blinkit (Zomato-owned) has a fast, 3-round process
Story-based DSA problems — decode the DP/graph pattern from the story
System Design focuses on practical architecture — SQL vs NoSQL, indexing
They value speed — show you can deliver under pressure
Short interview cycle — usually 1-2 weeks from first call to offer

do: Read DSA problem stories carefully — hidden patterns
dont: Ignore the story context in DSA problems
